heading: Projects & Files
intro: Projects give Claude persistent context so you don't have to re-explain your world every conversation.
what_is_it: A Project is a workspace where you upload reference files, set standing instructions, and have multiple conversations sharing the same background. Think of it as a briefing pack that stays available across every chat within that Project.

comparison_rows.project: Claude needs your terminology, style, or templates

comparison_rows.regular: You won't refer back to this
comparison_rows.project: You want to build on previous work
Recurring reporting, client work, or team tasks

steps: Click Projects in the left sidebar, then New Project.
Give it a clear name (e.g. "Q4 Board Reporting", "Client ABC", "Monthly Close Process").
Add Project Instructions. This is the standing brief Claude references in every conversation within the Project.
Upload reference files: templates, past reports, style guides, org charts, glossaries.
Start a conversation inside the Project. Claude has access to everything you've uploaded.

instruction_examples.title: Finance team example
instruction_examples.body:
You are helping the Finance team at Inizio with recurring reporting and analysis.

Key context:
- Fiscal year: January to December
- Reporting currency: GBP
- Three business units: Consulting, Clinical, and Digital
- Board meets quarterly; leadership team meets monthly
- ERP: NetSuite

When writing commentary or reports:
- Professional, concise language. No filler.
- Always include variance vs budget and vs prior year
- Flag items more than 10% off budget
- Use the format from the template files unless told otherwise

When working with data files:
- Look for anomalies before I ask
- If numbers don't add up or look unusual, flag them proactively
- Don't round aggressively. Keep to nearest thousand in summaries.

instruction_examples.title: Management / strategy example
instruction_examples.body:
You are supporting the Strategy and Transformation team at Inizio.

Key context:
- Healthcare services company, UK/Ireland/US operations
- Strategic plan 2024-2027, three pillars: Growth, Efficiency, People
- 12 transformation workstreams, each with a named lead
- I report to the COO

Preferences:
- Structured output: headings, bullets, tables
- Lead with "so what" before the detail
- When reviewing proposals, be critical. Challenge what might be wrong.
- Match the tone and format of the example documents uploaded to this Project

idea_cards.title: Board reporting
idea_cards.body: Upload your board pack template, previous commentary, and data formats. Each month, start a new conversation and attach the latest data.

idea_cards.title: Client engagement
idea_cards.body: Upload the proposal, SOW, key correspondence, and templates. Use for all work on that client.

idea_cards.title: Budget cycle
idea_cards.body: Upload the timetable, submission template, prior year actuals, and guidelines. Use for reviewing submissions and drafting challenges.

idea_cards.title: Personal productivity
idea_cards.body: Upload your writing style examples, meeting note format, preferred email structure. Claude adapts to how you work.

file_cards.title: Tips for better results
items: Scanned PDFs are harder for Claude to read
For Excel, make sure data has clear headers
Tell Claude which sheet or section to focus on
Name files clearly so you can reference them
